entities.name: GRAIN LEGUME
entities.description: Leguminous crops grown for their seeds

entities.name: DRYLAND CEREAL
entities.description: Cereal crops grown in arid or semi-arid regions

entities.name: SEED SYSTEMS
entities.description: The set of activities and institutions that ensure the availability and delivery of seeds

entities.name: COMMODITY VALUE CHAINS
entities.description: The full range of activities required to bring a commodity from production to final consumption

entities.name: DRYLANDS
entities.description: Arid or semi-arid regions characterized by low rainfall

entities.name: NON-IMPROVED SEED
entities.description: Seeds that have not been genetically improved for higher yield or resistance

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of output per unit of input in agricultural production

entities.name: POVERTY LINE
entities.description: The minimum level of income deemed adequate in a given country

entities.name: GENETIC GAINS
entities.description: The increase in the average performance of a crop population due to genetic improvement

entities.name: SEED DELIVERY SYSTEMS
entities.description: The mechanisms used to distribute seeds to farmers

relationships.description: Strategy focuses on seed systems for grain legumes
Strategy focuses on seed systems for dryland cereals
Seed delivery systems are driven through commodity value chains
Use of non-improved seed results in lower productivity
Smallholders in drylands use non-improved seed
Farmers in drylands often live below the poverty line
Seed systems strategy aims to deliver realized genetic gains
